sessions.name: Joy
sessions.summary:
Joy, a cat, presented for acute vomiting with possible hair tie ingestion. Exam showed mild abdominal pain on palpation, guarding, normal mucous membranes, slight tachycardia, and dehydration not noted; weight 4.45 kg, T 100.9°F, HR 180, RR 32. Assessment lists GI upset from dietary indiscretion with potential for gastritis or early GI foreign body/ pancreatitis. Plan included abdominal radiographs now with potential ultrasound if needed, subcutaneous LRS 100 mL, and Cerenia 1 mg/kg SQ given in hospital; at home, Ondansetron 2 mg PO BID for 3-5 days. Start bland diet in small portions q4-6h. If vomiting >2 times/24h or anorexia >12h, imaging indicated; seek emergency care if lethargy or abdominal pain develops. Owner agreed to radiographs, Cerenia, and SQ fluids today; ultrasound declined. Follow-up recheck in 24-48 hours or sooner if vomiting returns.**
}

sessions.name: Joy
sessions.summary: Joy presented for acute vomiting since last night with possible hair tie ingestion. Exam showed mild mid-cranial abdominal pain with guarding, HR 180, RR 32, temp 100.9 F, mildly tense but hydrated. Diagnoses include GI upset from dietary indiscretion, gastritis, potential early/linear foreign body concerns, and pancreatitis. Plan included abdominal radiographs and SQ fluids (LRS 100 mL); medications given: Cerenia 1 mg/kg SQ once; home meds: Ondansetron ODT 2 mg PO every 12 hours for 3-5 days. Begin bland diet in small portions every 4-6 hours. Monitor for vomiting; recheck in 24-48 hours or sooner if vomiting returns; imaging if appetite loss persists or symptoms worsen. Ultrasound declined for now; radiograph results to be reviewed via clinic call. No vaccines, no at-home meds beyond prescribed; owner approved radiographs and treatments today.
